meta.title: HVAC - BMW Project | Augusto Galves
meta.description: Technical details of the exhaust and air conditioning project for BMW.

card1.title: Thermal control in hot climate
card1.desc: External temperature up to 42 °C in Rio de Janeiro

card1.title: LG VRF System
card1.desc: Multiple evaporators with zone control

context.title: Project Context
context.content: HVAC project for a BMW dealership with distinct areas, including a vehicle workshop and customer service spaces.

desafio.title: Challenge
desafio.content: Guarantee thermal comfort and air quality in a large volume warehouse with high thermal load and emissions, without exorbitant costs.

solucao.title: Developed Solution
solucao.content: Combination of VRF system, splits and dedicated mechanical ventilation/exhaust to efficiently serve each environment.
list: Thermal load calculation done with Carrier HAP software
Mechanical air renewal with filtration and controlled diffusion
Thermal load (excluding workshop): 43.1 TR
Workshop thermal load: 21.6 TR

results.title: Results
results.content: The system guarantees stable thermal comfort, air renewal according to standards and efficient gas removal, providing operational safety and a better experience for both customers and the technical team.
list: Environments maintained between 22–23 °C even under high external load
Air quality in compliance with ANVISA and ABNT standards
Thermal comfort in the workshop area

cta.title: Did you like the project?
cta.subtitle: Get in touch to discuss ideas or opportunities.

meta.title: HVAC Executive Project - AGCO Academy | Augusto Galves
meta.description: Corporate HVAC executive project for AGCO Academy. Focus on energy efficiency, thermal comfort and compliance with ABNT and ANVISA standards.

card1.title: Huge project area
card1.desc: About 3,719 m² air-conditioned.

card3.title: Pressure drop in ducts
card3.desc: Very extensive ducts have reduced efficiency.

card1.title: Equipment Selection
card1.desc: Splits compatible with large lengths of refrigerant piping.

card2.title: Re-use
card2.desc: Integration of existing equipment to reduce costs.

card3.title: Network Optimization
card3.desc: Division of outdoor air and exhaust systems for lower pressure drop.

context.title: Project Overview
context.content: Executive air conditioning and mechanical ventilation project for AGCO Academy, in Jundiaí-SP, developed in partnership with AKMX Architecture and Engineering. The scope covers warehouse 2 and the administrative building, with full sizing of the HVAC systems.

desafio.title: The Challenge
desafio.content: Serve environments with distinct occupancy profiles in more than 3,700 m², with extensive networks of ducts and refrigerant piping far from the condensing units, respecting length limits and controlling pressure drops.

solucao.title: Developed Solution
solucao.content: System based on 23 splits for large lengths of piping, with re-use of equipment and optimized mechanical ventilation.
list: Thermal load calculation via Carrier HAP software
Re-use of 7 existing equipment units (3 relocated and 4 maintained)
Dedicated high-capacity split for the auditorium with a mechanical room
Duct networks sized for low pressure drop

results.title: Results
results.content: The system guarantees stable thermal comfort and efficient operation, meeting normative requirements and the physical limitations of the project.
list: Environments maintained between 22–23 °C
Pressure drop control in extensive networks
Cost reduction with equipment re-use

cta.title: Did you like the project?
cta.subtitle: Get in touch to discuss ideas or opportunities.

meta.title: HVAC Retrofit - NEOGÁS Corporate Headquarters | Augusto Galves
meta.description: HVAC Retrofit executive project for NEOGÁS headquarters in Rio de Janeiro.

card1.title: Asset Integration
card1.desc: Compatibility of new, relocated and maintained systems in the same ecosystem.

card2.title: Compliance and IAQ
card2.desc: Ensure healthiness and 1,650 m³/h of air renewal at the ANVISA standard.

card3.title: Silent Operation
card3.desc: Rigorous mechanical attenuation to maintain a high-level corporate standard.

card1.title: Thermal load calculation
card1.desc: Thermal load calculation using Carrier HAP software.

card2.title: T.A.B. Protocols
card2.desc: Testing, Adjusting, and Balancing procedures detailed in the project.

card3.title: Optimized Diffusion
card3.desc: Distribution via TDC ducts with precision control dampers.

context.title: Project Strategic Vision
context.content: Executive Air Conditioning Retrofit Project (HVAC) for the 13th floor of the NEOGÁS corporate headquarters, in downtown Rio de Janeiro. The scope required a high-level technical approach to modernize the thermal infrastructure, ensuring operational continuity and promoting the intelligent re-use of existing assets to optimize investments.

desafio.title: The Challenge
desafio.content: The main obstacle was to carry out the retrofit in a high-standard corporate environment, integrating new units with relocated and maintained equipment, without sacrificing energy efficiency or Indoor Air Quality (IAQ). The compatibility required absolute precision to simultaneously meet ANVISA guidelines and acoustic and thermal comfort requirements.

solucao.title: Engineering Solution
solucao.content: The application of engineering intelligence allowed the readjustment of the thermal park through rigorous simulations in the Carrier HAP v4.90 software. The project foresaw the integration of 7 new inverter systems, the relocation of 2 units and the strategic maintenance of 6 existing machines, ensuring the best cost-benefit (CAPEX/OPEX) for the client.
list: Advanced thermal simulation via Carrier HAP v4.90 for capacity validation.
Design of galvanized steel duct network optimized for low pressure drop (SMACNA).
Integration of in-line micro fans with G1/M5 filter boxes for outdoor air supply.
Specification of TAB (Testing, Adjusting, and Balancing) routines for commissioning validation.

results.title: Impact and Results
results.content: Delivering an executive project focused on failure mitigation significantly reduced risks and installation costs. The system guarantees occupational well-being and meets all the health demands required in the current corporate scenario.
list: Proven CAPEX reduction through the justified re-use of existing machinery.
Guarantee of 1,650 m³/h of renewed and filtered outdoor air for the floor.
Thermally stable system at 22.0°C, ensuring comfort in critical areas like the Staff (5.26 TR).

cta.title: Did you like the project?

meta.title: MEP Project - Beach House | Augusto Galves
meta.description: Electrical installations project (MEP) for a high-end residence. Focus on safety, panel detailing (QDC) and compliance with IECC 2023.

card1.title: Limited Budget
card1.desc: Maximize quality without exceeding the cost defined by the client.

card2.title: Existing Masonry
card2.desc: Masonry already executed, requiring adaptation of solutions without demolitions.

card3.title: Field Execution
card3.desc: Reduce errors with clear technical detailing to guide the construction site.

context.title: Project Vision
context.content: Executive MEP installations project for a residence in São Pedro da Aldeia–RJ. The focus was to align the aesthetics of a beach house with a low execution cost, maintaining safety and functionality.

desafio.title: The Challenge
desafio.content: Elevate quality without exceeding the budget, with the masonry already executed. The solutions needed to adapt to the existing structure, avoiding demolitions and layout limitations.

solucao.title: Adopted Solution
solucao.content: Detailed executive project to reduce improvisation on site, with solutions adapted to the existing structure and a focus on simple execution.
list: Technical detailing with guidance per construction stage
Distribution of points and circuits compatible with existing masonry
Layout resolved with strategic use of furniture
Standardization to facilitate execution and reduce errors

results.title: Impact and Results
results.content: The detailing of the project reduced improvisations on site and allowed for more controlled execution, even with cost limitations and existing structure.
list: Less need for adjustments in the field
Execution guided by clear instructions in the project
Compatibility with existing masonry without demolitions

meta.title: Autoclick - Piano Tiles | Augusto Galves
meta.description: Development of an autoclick bot in Python using real-time image processing to break records in Piano Tiles.

card1.title: Screen capture FPS
card1.desc: Capture and analyze the highest amount of frames per second.

card2.title: Identification Accuracy
card2.desc: Zero out the number of false positives in identification due to the blur of the keys at high speed

card3.title: Click Latency
card3.desc: Deal with game acceleration, click latency, game engine latency, and script latency.

card1.title: Capturing only a fraction of the screen
card1.desc: Capturing only one rectangle, and analyzing only 1 pixel in the row of each key.

card2.title: Consistent recognition
card2.desc: Minimum color intervals to trigger the click. Only shades very close to black.

card3.title: Y-Offset
card3.desc: Anticipation of reading coordinates based on the current speed of the loop.

context.title: Project Strategic Vision
context.content: Development of a Python script focused on high performance and ultra-low latency. The technical goal was to create an algorithm capable of "playing" Piano Tiles at superhuman speeds, utilizing real-time matricial image processing and direct integration with the operating system for mechanical click simulation.

desafio.title: The Challenge
desafio.content: The game accelerates exponentially, making conventional automation approaches obsolete due to processing overhead. The algorithm needed to capture the screen, transform the visual data into computational matrices, analyze color limits (thresholding), and fire hardware commands — all in the range of milliseconds, before the game key disappeared from the screen.

solucao.title: Solution
solucao.content: The architected solution did not focus on increasing computational power, but on anticipating the system's behavior. To compensate for the speed increase (and the inherent processing latency), I programmed a dynamic variation in the vertical displacement (Y-offset). As the loop advanced, the bot "looked" further up the screen, intercepting the target at the perfect time.
list: Screen conversion via PIL into structured NumPy arrays for optimized pixel extraction.
Modulation of repetition loops, dynamically altering the scanning coordinates according to acceleration (Slow, Fast, and Super Fast Modes).
Low-level interaction with `win32api` guaranteeing a click response time (`0.001s`) straight to the hardware.

results.title: Impact and Results
results.content: This project is a proof of concept that understanding the physical limits of hardware and language (Python) allows bypassing performance bottlenecks with logical creativity.
list: Registered record of processing and screen response: 28,927 keys mapped and clicked per second.
Stability in a "Hyper Speed" environment, without overloading memory or causing OS crashes.
Practical proof of knowledge in matrix manipulation and native Windows APIs.

meta.title: Ultimate Belt Design | Augusto Galves
meta.description: Patented engineering software for belt sizing. Includes automated calculations and full system validation.

card1.title: Manual Iterations
card1.desc: Belt sizing requires multiple catalog consultations and dozens of interpolations.

card2.title: Literary Inconsistency
card2.desc: Identification of inaccuracies in the standard bibliography.

context.title: Precision Engineering and Automation
context.content: Modern engineering demands speed without giving up technical rigor. The Ultimate Belt Design and Analysis 2026 is a patented software developed to eliminate repetitive human effort in the design of machine elements.

academic_validation.title: Technical Audit: Correcting the Literature
academic_validation.content: To guarantee the reliability of the algorithm, I conducted a thorough review of Chapter 17 of the renowned book 'Shigley's Mechanical Engineering Design'. I identified several inconsistencies in the official resolutions of the literature.
list: Premise Failures Identified: linear velocity limits and vibration
Accuracy Validation and implementation of safety locks
Scientific Production documenting the development and audit

desafio.title: The Design Challenge
desafio.content: Manual sizing involves extensive consultations with physical catalogs, interpolations susceptible to errors, and complex fatigue calculations. The goal was to transform hours of iterative calculations and safety validations into an execution of a few seconds.

results.title: Results and Delivery
results.content: The tool delivers sizing instantly. The main impact is the mitigation of human mathematical error and savings on repairs due to the correct specification of components.

meta.title: Personal Portfolio Website - Vanilla Web Dev | Augusto Galves
meta.description: Full-stack development from scratch of my portfolio website. Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS architecture, deployment via GitHub Pages, Cloudflare management and extreme SEO optimization.

dev-portfolio-website.title: Web Development - Personal Portfolio
dev-portfolio-website.tech: Vanilla JS · GitHub Pages · Cloudflare Domains

card1.title: Zero Frameworks
card1.desc: Build a scalable application using exclusively vanilla HTML/CSS/JS.

card2.title: Modularity
card2.desc: Develop a system of templates and Web Components without external libraries like React.

card3.title: Integrated Infrastructure
card3.desc: Configure domain, CDN, security, and professional e-mail from scratch via DNS managers.

card1.title: Custom JS Components
card1.desc: Architecture based on native components for maximum performance and reusability.

card2.title: Cloudflare & Deploy
card2.desc: Continuous integration (CI/CD) with GitHub Pages and advanced routing through Cloudflare.

card3.title: Extreme Optimization
card3.desc: Code without redundancies, maximum page-speed, and high SEO metrics.

context.title: Web Engineering from Scratch
context.content: To establish my digital presence solidly, I decided to design and develop this portfolio site by programming from scratch, without the help of any framework. The project was executed between December 2025 and April 2026, with over 200 commits on GitHub. The focus was to have total control of the infrastructure and ensure it is fully optimized.

desafio.title: The Technical Challenge
desafio.content: The most critical decision was to forego modern frameworks (like React, Vue, or Tailwind). The challenge was to build a modular, scalable, and extremely fast system, using exclusively native web technologies.

solucao.title: Architecture and Implementation
solucao.content: I developed a robust solution focused on the fundamentals, ranging from the creation of logical components to the management of DNS records, guaranteeing a professional and secure digital presence.
list: Custom Web Components: Creation of reusable JS components
Exclusive UI/UX Design with a personal exclusive icon library
SEO & Performance with integration of JSON-LD and Open Graph
Infrastructure with Cloudflare and professional e-mail configured via DNS redirection

results.title: Results
results.content: The result is a personal branding platform of the highest technological performance. The absence of third-party dependencies ensures that the site will never become obsolete due to framework updates.
list: Loading time and FCP with maximum score on Google Lighthouse
Practical proof of fluency in front-end algorithms and infrastructure management
Modular code base ready for quick expansion of new projects

cta.subtitle: Explore the public repository on GitHub or get in touch to discuss digital solutions.
